description: A carved stone capital from a Romanesque church showing three frontal human figures in shallow relief. The central figure faces directly forward with hands clasped or holding an object at chest level. The two flanking figures turn slightly inward. All three have simplified, rounded features with large eyes and schematic facial details. The carving is deeply cut in reddish-brown stone with a slightly weathered surface. Above the figures, the capital flares outward to meet the architectural member it supports.

_meta.title: Capital, Church of St. Christopher of Rio Mau
